data.title: CARsgen Therapeutics Holdings reports H1 results
data.description:
• CARsgen Therapeutics Holdings press release (OTCPK:CRTHF):  revenue was around RMB51 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025.
  • Gross profit was around RMB29 million for the six months ended June 30, 2025.
  • Cash and bank balances were around RMB1,261 million as of June 30, 2025. Cash and cash equivalents and deposits at the end of 2025 are expected to be not less than RMB1,100 million. The company expects to have adequate cash into the 2028 excluding subsequent cash inflows.

data.title: Catheter Precision executes 1-for-19 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Catheter Precision (NYSE:VTAK) implemented a 1-for-19 reverse stock split effective August 15, 2025.
  • Each 19 shares of common stock were automatically converted into 1 share, with no change in par value.
  • Total outstanding shares reduced from 23.32M to ~1.66M.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: U.S. Import prices rise more than expected in July; export prices inch up
data.description: U.S. import prices gained 0.4% M/M in July, stronger than than the 0.1% increase expected and the prior month's 0.1% decrease, which was revised from +0.1%, according to data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on Friday.

data.title: July retail sales rise slightly less than expected, core sales in line
data.description:
U.S. retail sales: +0.5% M/M to $726.3B vs. +0.6% consensus and +0.9% prior (revised from +0.6%), according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Friday.

Y/Y, +3.92% vs. +4.35% prior (revised from +3.9%).

Core retail sales (excluding motor vehicles and parts): +0.3% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.8% prior (revised from +0.5%).

Retail sales, excluding autos and gas, +0.2% M/M vs. +0.8% prior (revised from +0.6%).

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: Centrica signs 10-year U.S. natural gas deal with Devon Energy
data.description:
British Gas owner Centrica (OTCPK:CPYYF) (OTCPK:CPYYY) said Friday it entered into a natural gas agreement with Devon Energy (NYSE:DVN), which will supply 50K MMBtu/day of natural gas - the equivalent to five liquefied natural gas cargoes per year - over a 10‑year term starting in 2028; the volumes will be indexed to the TTF European benchmark price.

The deal expands Centrica's (OTCPK:CPYYF) (OTCPK:CPYYY) global gas business after recently opening a New York office, and "supports Centrica’s objective of managing market price risk in its LNG portfolio by aligning feed gas pricing with European gas prices whilst providing Devon Energy with international price exposure."

Centrica (OTCPK:CPYYF) (OTCPK:CPYYY) signed a 15-year agreement in February to supply American LNG to Petrobras, and last year it struck a deal with U.S.-based Coterra Energy to secure more gas.

data.title: Bread Financial July net charge-offs improve, while delinquency rate ticks up
data.description:
Bread Financial's (NYSE:BFH) credit card delinquency rate ticked up, while its net loss rate edged down in July, the company said on Friday.

The credit card issuer's delinquency rate increased to 5.8%  in July from 5.7% in June, but remains below its 6.2% level notched in July 2024. The latest reading compares with its pre-pandemic print of 5.5% in July 2019.

Its net loss rate of 7.6% improved from 7.8% in the prior month and 8.0% a year ago. 

Period-end credit card and other loans of $17.6B fell 0.6% from June and the year-ago level.

data.title: Flowers Foods lowers outlook again amid shifting consumer tastes, heightened competition
data.description:
Flower Foods (NYSE:FLO) faced ongoing headwinds in the second quarter as consumers moved away from carbohydrates, while broader economic uncertainty and intensifying competition squeezed profits and prompted another cut to full-year guidance.

As a result, Flower Foods (NYSE:FLO) shares are down as much as 5% into Friday’s open.

To address the macroeconomic and retail environment, Flower Foods’ CEO said the company is “proactively investing in innovation and M&A to transform our portfolio and align it with consumer demand for better-for-you and value-oriented products.”

“These differentiated products enable us to target attractive opportunities in our existing categories, while expanding into adjacencies and new categories with exciting growth prospects.” CEO Ryals McMullian said.  

The parent company of TastyKake and Wonder Bread now expects revenue growth of   2.7% to 4.0%, representing a range of $5.239B to $5.308B versus the prior guidance of 3.8% to 5.7% growth, or $5.297B to $5.395B, with the new range below the consensus estimate of $5.31B.

Adjusted earnings are now expected to be between $1.00 to $1.10 per share versus $1.05 to $1.15 per share, previously, with the midpoint of the new guidance below the $1.09 consensus estimate. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between $512M and $538M from the prior range of $534M to $562M.

For the second quarter, Flower Foods (NYSE:FLO) earned a profit of $0.30 per share, up $0.06 from a year ago and a penny better than estimates. Adjusted EBITDA was down 4.0% to $137.7M, representing 11.1% of sales, a 60 basis points decline.

Net sales were up 1.5% to $1.243B, missing expectations by $30M.

data.title: Cosan reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Cosan press release (NYSE:CSAN): Q2 net loss of R$946 million, a reduction of R$719 million compared to 2Q24.
  • EBITDA reached R$1.2 billion in the quarter, a 12% decrease versus 2Q24, reflecting one-off effects that positively impacted the result in the same period last year.
  • Year-to-date, EBITDA grew 11%, supported by higher volumes, a richer mix in the distribution segment, and Edge’s improved performance.

data.title: Nexxen seeks approval for $20M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Nexxen International (NASDAQ:NEXN) plans to launch a new $20M share repurchase program after completing its current program, pending a 30-day creditor objection period and lender approval.
  • As of July 31, 2025, ~$7.2 million remained under the company’s current ordinary share repurchase authorization, which is expected to be completed prior to its scheduled end date of November 19, 2025.
  • Nexxen will also invest $35M to raise its stake in VIDAA to ~6%, supporting its North American CTV expansion. The company continues to invest in team growth, product innovation, and potential targeted strategic opportunities to expand data assets, enhance AI capabilities, and grow in high-potential markets.

data.title: Schrodinger downgraded at Citi after clinical program exit
data.description:
Citi downgraded Schrodinger (NASDAQ:SDGR) to Neutral from Buy after the AI-focused biotech announced its decision to discontinue a clinical program for a leukemia candidate following the emergence of unexpected adverse events.

Schrodinger (NASDAQ:SDGR), based in New York, announced Thursday it would halt the clinical development of SGR-2921, targeted at relapsed/refractory acute myeloid leukemia or high-risk myelodysplastic syndromes.

The decision followed two AML patient deaths, attributed to the experimental therapy in a Phase 1 trial.

“Given recent developments in the company’s internally developed drug pipeline as well as continued headwinds facing the biotech/pharma industries, we are downgrading Schrödinger,” Bloomberg News reported, quoting Citi analyst David Lebowitz.

With a $20 per share target on SDGR, the analyst added that the company’s recent pipeline updates have “fallen short, raising concerns on the potential of Schrödinger’s proprietary drug portfolio efforts.”

Lebowitz also argued that ongoing macro headwinds have impacted the company’s software segment, weighing on Schrodinger’s (NASDAQ:SDGR) topline growth.
